title: rosa subolida var. genuina
intro: Rosa subolida var. genuina, also known as the Sweetbriar or Briar Rose, is a type of flowering shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in its leaves and flowers. The medicinal properties of Rosa subolida var. genuina have been found to have various health benefits, including wound healing, relief from fever and rheumatism, and even potential antiviral effects against certain viral infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
